items.title: RT SOIC Research: Today we are going to discuss a very unique 2W OEM that has been able to built a product that is built to last in 10+ years. For con...
items.description_html:
RT SOIC Research
Today we are going to discuss a very unique 2W OEM that has been able to built a product that is built to last in 10+ years. For context - They had no legacy nothing and have built the business ground up from nothing (product first distribution later)

Ather invested in the things that compound and are hard to copy i.e. design IP, software, brand, charging network and rented the things that don't (cell manufacturing). That is why it spends less than Ola yet has better unit economics, and why the moat is earned rather than bought.

And this is the fruits of investing in building technology and a superior product for years before just thinking about scaling distribution. At the end of the day we can see the result that the product won that did not catch fire :)

But here today we will get into details of how they created this MOAT and how it is now able to win a superior market share and show such a strong growth at the same time when competition did not decrease in fact it has gone up!

items.title: RT Saurabh Chandra: It was a great show at Automate for @atirobotics. There is a clear vibe shift in robotics demand in US manufacturing. There is a h...
items.description_html:
RT Saurabh Chandra
It was a great show at Automate for @atirobotics. There is a clear vibe shift in robotics demand in US manufacturing. There is a huge intent to get robots now compared to earlier when people were evaluating robots.

While our pallet mover and Tugger were our workhorses with folks looking to deploy immediately, the show stealer was our industrial humanoid, Sherpa Mecha. Many people were surprised to see we did the actuators and had a very novel arm design (motors in shoulder, cable driven, carbon fiber arms) compared to most folks who have put together Chinese arms with software on top. All our products are made with first principles thinking and this is no different. Focused usecases and real deployments will win the market.

We will get to real deployments much faster (next summer) and beta deployments start this year itself. We already have a global customer in both US and India where we will be deploying the Mecha.

We also move into our new facility to assemble the robots near Detroit in July.

Never been a better time to be in robotics. Super excited!

items.title: India's need for robotics is just as high, not because of a population decline, but because of upward mobility. Factories that depend too much on work...
items.description_html:
India's need for robotics is just as high, not because of a population decline, but because of upward mobility. Factories that depend too much on workers are plagued with political issues, worker holiday schedules, workplace safety violations (by workers themselves!) and the need to keep training and hiring as people move jobs often (unless you're located in a village where there's nothing else)

We haven't done it mostly because the thought process is that cost is too high initially. That's also why the robots aren't even made in India - but that area will balloon massively. It won't be population issues, it will be cost - as workers get more expensive, we will move to robots, and produce 10x more.


Shankar Sharma: And now, @FT writes an article on June 25th, on how China is beating the population decline, by massive use of robotics.

Precisely what I wrote in our Business Standard piece last week.

Ab to maan lo, nalaykon, ki apun Bhiku Mhatre hai...

After nearly three years of pressure, the Indian cotton yarn industry is seeing a meaningful reset, driven as much by supply correction as demand recovery.

Key takeaways:

- Structural capacity rationalisation: ~20% of spinning capacity (10–12 mn spindles) has exited, tightening supply and improving pricing power for organised players.

- Demand revival with global linkages: Stronger export flows, especially China resurgence, easing U.S. tariff-related uncertainties and improved sentiment around trade agreements are supporting order books.

- Margin recovery underway: Yarn spreads have rebounded to ~₹120–125/kg, with operating margins expected to expand by 150–200 bps in FY27.

items.title: China’s is seeing unprecedented money supply growth: China's M2 money supply is up to a record ~240% of GDP, the highest among any major economy in t...
items.description_html:
China’s is seeing unprecedented money supply growth:

China's M2 money supply is up to a record ~240% of GDP, the highest among any major economy in the world.

This metric has surged +100 percentage points since the 2008 Financial Crisis.

Over this period, China’s M2 money supply has surged +500% in Dollar terms.

By comparison, the country's gold reserves and total FX reserves have risen +100% and +60%, respectively.

To put this into perspective, Japan's M2-to-GDP ratio, the next highest, stands at ~185%, while the US sits at ~70%.

China's monetary expansion is unlike anything seen among major economies.

items.description_html:
Fear is building beneath the market’s surface:

The Fear & Greed Index is now down to 24.8, the lowest since early April.

This metric is now below the 30-point threshold, signaling "Extreme Fear" among investors.

The reading comes despite the S&P 500 sitting just -3.7% below its all-time high.

By comparison, the index bottomed at 5.8 points on March 30th and 5.2 points in November 2024.

This comes as the 5-day average put-to-call ratio is up to 0.84, the highest since the 2nd week of April and above the levels seen between July 2025 and January 2026.

Keep watching sentiment.

Total consumer credit surged +$25 billion in March, to a record $5.14 trillion.

This marks the largest monthly increase since March 2025.

Revolving credit, which includes credit cards, jumped +$10 billion, to $1.34 trillion, the highest since November 2024.

This was also the biggest monthly increase since February 2024.

Non-revolving credit, mainly auto and student loans, rose +$15 billion, to a record $3.80 trillion.

As a result, total consumer credit has now surged +$1.05 trillion since the 2020 pandemic.

US consumers' debt levels are skyrocketing.

items.description_html:
AI is reshaping how institutional investors work:

~52% of institutional investors now primarily use AI for research, according to a Barclays survey of 410 fixed-income investors.

This is followed by hedge funds, at ~44%, which primarily use AI to process and analyze large volumes of market data.

By comparison, ~27% of hedge funds use AI for modelling and risk analysis, versus ~22% of long-only managers and ~17% of asset owners.

Operations, compliance and reporting, and investment decisions each account for just 10% to 15% across these groups.

AI is changing how investment decisions are made.

items.title: Volatility among tech stocks is historically elevated: The gap between the Nasdaq 100 volatility index, $VXN, and the volatility index, $VIX, is up to...
items.description_html:
Volatility among tech stocks is historically elevated:

The gap between the Nasdaq 100 volatility index, $VXN, and the volatility index, $VIX, is up to 12 points, the highest in at least 23 years.

The difference has more than tripled since the start of May.

This comes as $VXN surged +9 points or +43% over this period, while $VIX rose just +2 points, or +9% at the same time.

By comparison, during the 2008 Financial Crisis and the 2020 pandemic, the gap peaked at 7 and 11 points, respectively.

Put simply, investors are pricing in significantly more uncertainty for technology stocks than for the broader market.

Market volatility is here to stay.

items.title: The US housing market is now firmly in favor of buyers: 46% of home sellers offered concessions to buyers in May, the highest May reading on record. T...
items.description_html:
The US housing market is now firmly in favor of buyers:

46% of home sellers offered concessions to buyers in May, the highest May reading on record.

This percentage has DOUBLED since 2022 and also marks the 2nd consecutive annual increase.

This comes as the US now has ~47% more home sellers than buyers, near the highest on record, with elevated mortgage rates and economic uncertainty continuing to weigh on buyer demand.

In Nashville, 76% of sellers gave concessions in May, the highest among the 28 major metropolitan areas.

This was followed by Charlotte and Atlanta, at 71% and 69%, respectively.

By comparison, just 3% of New York home sellers offered concessions last month.

Homebuyers are gaining negotiating power across much of the US housing market.

items.title: South Korean investors have never borrowed this much to buy equities: Margin loans in South Korea are up to a record ~$26 billion, DOUBLING since the ...
items.description_html:
South Korean investors have never borrowed this much to buy equities:

Margin loans in South Korea are up to a record ~$26 billion, DOUBLING since the start of 2025.

However, as a % of Korea's free float, the portion of the market value available for public trading, margin loans are down to ~0.8%, the lowest since the 2020 pandemic low.

This comes as the surge in market cap has significantly outpaced the growth in leverage.

Meanwhile, during the recent market pullbacks, the daily forced liquidation ratio spiked to 4-5% of total outstanding margin loans, well above the ~1% seen under normal conditions.

This means brokers were forced to liquidate 4%-5% of all margin-backed positions in a single day because borrowers could not meet their margin calls.

Record leverage is exacerbating South Korea's market volatility.

items.description_html:
BREAKING: China imported +163 tonnes of gold in May, the largest monthly import since March 2024.

This marks the 3rd consecutive month with gold imports exceeding +150 tonnes.

As a result, in the first 5 months of 2026, total gold imports rose +76% YoY, to +692 tonnes.

This has been driven by strong retail demand for physical bullion bars and gold accumulation plans, low-cost products that allow investors to build up gold holdings gradually over time.

Meanwhile, the People's Bank of China added +10 tonnes of gold to its official gold reserves in May, its 19th consecutive monthly purchase, bringing total holdings to a record 2,331 tonnes.

China’s demand for gold remains strong.

items.title: BREAKING: The US median home sales price jumped +$8,400 in May, to $424,900, the 2nd-highest since January 2025. Over the last 2 months, the median pr...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The US median home sales price jumped +$8,400 in May, to $424,900, the 2nd-highest since January 2025.

Over the last 2 months, the median price has soared +$31,800, the largest 2-month increase in 4 years.

Meanwhile, the average home sale price surged +$39,200 in May, to $540,600, the 2nd-highest on record.

As a result, the gap between the median and average home sales price is up to $115,700, or 27%.

By comparison, in May 2022, the gap was $79,500, or 19%.

This comes as a growing share of home sales is concentrated among the most expensive properties, lifting the average price faster than the median.

Housing affordability is somehow is still getting worse.

items.title: No sector in history has ever been this large: The Information Technology sector now accounts for a record 39% of the S&P 500's total market cap. This...
items.description_html:
No sector in history has ever been this large:

The Information Technology sector now accounts for a record 39% of the S&P 500's total market cap.

This percentage has more than doubled since the 2020 pandemic.

This figure is also now above the 2000 Dot-Com Bubble peak of ~33% and the ~31% peak reached by the Energy sector in the 1980s.

Including internet retailers and digital media platforms such as Amazon, $AMZN, and Netflix, $NFLX, tech now accounts for a record 50% of the S&P 500's market value.

By comparison, this same group accounted for just 29% of the index at the Dot-Com Bubble peak.

Tech is all that matters.

items.title: US tech fund flows are seeing extreme volatility: US technology sector funds posted -$15 billion in outflows this week, the largest weekly withdrawal ...
items.description_html:
US tech fund flows are seeing extreme volatility:

US technology sector funds posted -$15 billion in outflows this week, the largest weekly withdrawal in at least 2.5 years.

This exceeds the previous largest weekly outflow over this period by +100%.

This also comes after +$19 billion in inflows the prior week, also the largest weekly inflow in at least 2.5 years.

Furthermore, US equity funds saw an -$8.5 billion outflow this week, the largest weekly withdrawal in 3 months, following a record +$119 billion inflow.

Year-to-date, US equity funds have attracted +$332 billion in inflows, accounting for half of the +$661 billion total across all developed markets.

Investors are cashing out massive profits.

items.title: We are experiencing an unprecedented ETF boom: US-listed ETFs have attracted over +$1 trillion in inflows so far in 2026, on track to surpass a record...
items.description_html:
We are experiencing an unprecedented ETF boom:

US-listed ETFs have attracted over +$1 trillion in inflows so far in 2026, on track to surpass a record +$2 trillion by year-end.

That would mark the 4th consecutive annual increase in ETF inflows and exceed the 2025 record by +33%, or +$500 billion.

Meanwhile, 700 ETFs have launched year-to-date, on track for another annual record.

In June alone, 186 ETFs have debuted, the highest monthly total in history.

The ETF industry is expanding at a record pace.

items.description_html:

HDFC Bank legal review finds no evidence to substantiate former Chairman Atanu Chakraborty’s allegations reports @_RituSingh
* Review finds no basis for concerns raised in Atanu Chakraborty’s resignation statement
* Three-month review covered thousands of documents and interviews with independent directors and senior management

* No evidence found that Atanu Chakraborty raised ethical concerns on Dubai matter during his tenure

-Remains negative on aluminium names, flagging a potential 30–40% downside.

Key factors:
1️⃣ Primary market shortage may be overstated
• China is nearing its 45 mtpa capacity cap
• China’s secondary aluminium consumption:
2024: 12.7 mt , 2025: 13.35 mt
➡️ Primary deficit is increasingly being replenished by scrap/secondary supply.

2️⃣ Middle East disruptions look temporary, not structural
• ~2.2 mtpa of primary capacity impacted
• Qatar Aluminium & Alba supply can normalize relatively quickly
• EGA’s Al Taweelah remains the key outage risk
⚠️ Aluminium prices could be vulnerable to an ~$800/t correction as the war-risk premium unwinds

items.title: Max India: Building an Integrated Senior Care Ecosystem in India Max India, through Antara Senior Care, is positioning itself beyond a traditional sen...
items.description_html:
Max India: Building an Integrated Senior Care Ecosystem in India

Max India, through Antara Senior Care, is positioning itself beyond a traditional senior living player.

The company is gradually building an integrated ecosystem around elderly care — combining assisted living, emergency medical support, holistic wellness, insurance, financial literacy, quality standards and clinical governance.

One key initiative is the pilot of an integrated wellness clinic, where modern medicine for emergency care is being combined with Ayurveda, acupressure and other wellness practices.

This matters because senior care is not just about providing residences. As people age, their needs become multi-dimensional — frailty, muscle loss, fall risk, memory care, assisted care, emergency preparedness and emotional wellness all become important.

Max India is also working with government bodies and ministries on senior care advocacy, including submissions to NITI Aayog on insurance and standardisation. This shows that the company is not only building facilities, but also helping shape the broader senior care framework in India.

Another important milestone is Antara becoming the first assisted living provider in India to secure NABH care home accreditation, with 2 care homes now certified.

For Max India, the opportunity is clear:

Senior care = Living + Healthcare + Wellness + Insurance + Standards + Trust.

This integrated approach could help the company differentiate itself in India’s evolving senior care market and build a scalable ecosystem around ageing with dignity. 🏡

Europe is the key strategic angle here. Persistent has been looking for a complementary acquisition in Europe, and Nagarro helps solve that gap. The combined company will have more than $600 Mn REV from Europe, along with more than $1.7 Bn from North America and over $400 Mn from the rest of the world.

Deal is also looks EPS accretive. On an illustrative pro forma TTM basis, the combined entity would have REV of $2,795 Mn, EBITDA of $463 Mn, EBIT of $337 Mn & PAT of $215 Mn.

The transaction assumes total debt of nearly $1.5 Bn, including acquisition funding & refinancing Nagarro’s existing debt.

Another concern is margins. Nagarro’s reported CY25 EBITDA margin was 12%. After adjustments, the margin improves to around 14-15%.

items.title: India’s Automotive Electronics Market is emerging as one of the most attractive opportunities Market size is already above $12 Bn & is expected to gr...
items.description_html:
India’s Automotive Electronics Market is emerging as one of the most attractive opportunities

Market size is already above $12 Bn & is expected to grow at 20% CAGR

This is nearly 3x the growth rate of the Industry !

Vehicles are becoming more electronic, more connected & more software driven

In EVs, electronics already account for 35-45%+ of vehicle value, which makes this one of the most important value pools in the mobility ecosystem

What is driving this opportunity

1. EV adoption & electrification
EVs have 2-3x higher electronics content compared to traditional vehicles. As EV adoption rises, the demand for battery management systems, power electronics, digital dashboards & integrated control systems also rises sharply.

2. Software defined vehicles
Vehicles are now becoming more software led. Features like OTA updates, connected dashboards & centralized control systems are creating new opportunities.

3. Connected vehicles
Vehicles are increasingly becoming like connected devices. Telematics, remote diagnostics, 4G/5G connectivity & in car infotainment are becoming standard features.

4. Advanced safety and ADAS
Safety features are becoming a big growth driver. Features like lane assist, blind spot monitoring, cameras, radar & sensors are seeing higher adoption.

5. Localization & semiconductor ecosystem
India is trying to build more local capability in electronics & semiconductors through policy support.

Digital clusters, infotainment & e-cockpit is one of the most attractive sub segments within automotive electronics

Multiple adjacent segments are also opening up:

- ADAS & active safety : AEB, lane assist, cameras, radar & ultrasonic sensors
- EV power electronics : BMS, inverters & on board chargers
- Sensors : MEMS, LiDAR, radar, ultrasonic & position sensors
- Body electronics : keyless entry, power windows, ambient lighting & comfort electronics
- Telematics & connectivity : e-SIM, 4G/5G, fleet management & remote diagnostics
- ECUs & domain controllers : shift from distributed ECUs to domain & zonal control

The future of auto components will not only be about mechanical parts, but also about software, sensors, displays, connectivity, safety systems & integrated electronics.

items.title: The worst thing an investor can do is keep averaging a stock only because the valuation looks attractive, especially when the fundamentals are deterio...
items.description_html:
The worst thing an investor can do is keep averaging a stock only because the valuation looks attractive, especially when the fundamentals are deteriorating.

Blind averaging down is usually a combination of two behavioural biases:

Sunk cost fallacy - “I have already invested so much, so I must recover from this stock.”

Confirmation bias - “The stock is 40% below my purchase price, valuations are very attractive, and I will find reasons to justify holding it.”

But a cheap stock with deteriorating fundamentals is often not a bargain.

The question is never, “How far has this fallen from my buy price?”

The real question is: “Knowing what I know today, if I had no position, would I initiate a fresh position at this price, in this business, with these fundamentals?”

If the honest answer is no, averaging down is simply doubling a mistake.

Averaging only makes sense when the thesis is intact.

Otherwise, it is better to accept the mistake, protect capital and redeploy it into a better opportunity.

items.title: RT Jesús Fernández-Villaverde: This is a photo I took of Keynes’s bedroom at Charleston House, Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant’s farmhouse. Keynes w...
items.description_html:
RT Jesús Fernández-Villaverde
This is a photo I took of Keynes’s bedroom at Charleston House, Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant’s farmhouse. Keynes wrote The Economic Consequences of the Peace at this table, looking out over the pond (also notice Duncan Grant’s 1917 portrait of Keynes in the top left corner, painted in the garden).

Keynes liked the area so much that he later leased Tilton House, just down the lane.

As always, Keynes was a dominating figure. Rumor has it that he changed all the clocks in the house to his own eccentric time zone, “Charleston time,” which suited the household’s bohemian lifestyle. Whether this is true is hard to ascertain, but it fits the atmosphere quite well.

It was most interesting for me to be in the room where a book I teach every fall semester came to life. The curator, however, was intrigued by the fact that I seemed to care far more about Keynes than about Duncan Grant or Vanessa Bell. I apologized to her for my professional bias, pointing out that nobody is perfect!

Separately, he makes an important point on jobs -- While Indian banks may not see job cuts in the AI world as global lenders have warned, job growth will be below balance sheet growth, Vaswani said.

items.title: Pre-Market Update!! (29-06-2026) India builds, expands & restructures from highways in Guyana to nutraceuticals, infra trusts, pharma deals & auto dem...
items.description_html:
Pre-Market Update!! (29-06-2026)

India builds, expands & restructures from highways in Guyana to nutraceuticals, infra trusts, pharma deals & auto demergers.

• India's nutraceutical sector is poised for a remarkable surge, projected to nearly double to $57 billion by 2030, fuelled by a growing consumer shift toward preventive healthcare and wellness-driven lifestyles across urban and rural demographics.

• Indus Infra Trust's board has sanctioned a ₹3,563 crore term loan facility from Union Bank of India. The capital will be deployed to refinance existing debt obligations tied to the trust's acquired and pipeline infrastructure assets.

• Strides Pharma Science is divesting its majority stake in subsidiary Pivot Path for ₹1,000 million. Ascent Capital will inject ₹500 million as primary capital, while Vintage Classic participates as an additional investor in this strategic transaction.

• Pricol has greenlit a landmark demerger, spinning off its Driver Information & Connected Vehicle Solutions division into a separately listed entity Pricol Autotech Ltd. Shareholders receive one Autotech share per Pricol share held, creating two focused, independent listed businesses.

• Puravankara has inked a ₹1,000 crore Joint Development Agreement for a 6.4-acre parcel in Sarjapur, Bengaluru, targeting ~0.8 msft of residential saleable area. This bolsters its Q1FY27 pipeline to ₹5,200 crore across four deals spanning 41.93 acres.

• Likhitha Infrastructure has secured a significant international pipeline project worth ₹510 crore, awarded by CPECC Abu Dhabi. This overseas contract reinforces the company's expanding global footprint and growing presence in cross-border energy infrastructure development.

• Transrail Lighting has bagged fresh orders aggregating ₹459 crore, further bolstering its unexecuted order book. The contracts solidify the company's stature as a prominent Transmission & Distribution EPC player with an increasingly robust global operational presence.

• Ashoka Buildcon has clinched a highway construction contract worth USD 35.42 million in Guyana, marking a significant international win. The overseas project underscores the company's expanding global infrastructure capabilities beyond its established domestic highway execution track record.

items.description_html:
A number that surprises most people: fewer than 8% of Indians hold a passport. Roughly 110 million valid documents in a country of 1.4 billion.

And it maps almost perfectly to migration.

Kerala accounts for 11.7% of passports issued over the past decade;
Assam, 0.9%.

The passport isn't just a travel document, it's a geography of who leaves and who doesn't.

The free market is quietly dead. India just hasn't said it out loud.

Every major economy now picks winners with state money:

🇺🇸 US → CHIPS + IRA (~$1.4T)

🇨🇳 China → Made in China 2025

🇯🇵 Japan → ¥370T across 17 strategic sectors

🇮🇳 India → PLI across 14 sectors

The next decade isn't won by the cheapest factory.

It's won by whoever the government decides to subsidize. India's already at the table.

items.description_html:
India built a $5 trillion market. The big money still hasn't shown up.

Institutional investors hold just ~24% of India's market cap (OECD, 2025).

Compare:

🇯🇵 Japan: ~33%

🇹🇼 Chinese Taipei: ~31%

🇮🇳 India: ~24%

🇰🇷 Korea: ~20%

India's market is already worth ~135% of GDP deeper than Korea or China. Yet institutional ownership lags the leaders. The room left to run isn't price. It's ownership.

items.title: India is the largest crypto market in Asia. It doesn't feel like one. India took in ~$340B of crypto inflows in a year more than Korea, Japan, or anyo...
items.description_html:
India is the largest crypto market in Asia. It doesn't feel like one.

India took in ~$340B of crypto inflows in a year more than Korea, Japan, or anyone in the region.

Yet as a share of GDP, that's only ~8% near the bottom of Asia. Viet Nam, a fraction of the size, runs at ~47%.

India's scale comes from sheer population and a deep economy, not deep penetration. The runway is the gap between volume and adoption.

items.title: AI just dragged America's power grid back to the 1950s. US electricity demand growth by decade: 1950s (new appliances): 8.5% 1960s (air conditioning):...
items.description_html:
AI just dragged America's power grid back to the 1950s.

US electricity demand growth by decade:

1950s (new appliances): 8.5%

1960s (air conditioning): 7.4%

1990s: 2.4%

2010s (LEDs): 0.2%

2021–24: 0.9%

2025–30 (data centers): 5.7% ⚡

Two decades of flat demand, erased.

The data-center era is the biggest load shock in 70 years.

items.title: RT Shailendra J Singh: This is the part of the AI cycle where things start to get worrisome Capex commitments are unprecedented Funding of capital int...
items.description_html:
RT Shailendra J Singh
This is the part of the AI cycle where things start to get worrisome

Capex commitments are unprecedented

Funding of capital intensive business models is also unprecedented

So much Capex will surely lead to use of leverage to boost RoE somewhere in the capital stack

If a platform shift happens or technology leapfrogs (eg 10x better memory, or scale up of data centers in space, or even possibly non silicon chips? Yes some people are attempting these … 🤯) or even an over supply at some point .. that could make things complicated for those with big $ commitments

For this reason, it feels crazy that investors have rotated to this extent out of software into hardware/capex centric businesses

No question, even if there is some pain or correction … AI will be the biggest technology mega trend for our generation

items.description_html:
Yoga Bar's revenue jumped 83% to over Rs 200 crore in FY25, but its FY26 numbers do not appear in the annual report.

That's because ITC still owns a 47.5% stake in the company and it is not yet a wholly owned subsidiary.

ITC took control of Yoga Bar's board in April.


Madhav Chanchani: ITC's says consumer brands acquisitions like Yoga Bar and Prasuma are now collectively tracking Rs 1,350 crore in revenue.

This comes after HUL (Minimalist & Oziva) and Marico (Beardo, Plix) also said their startup acquisitions have crossed Rs 1,000 crore in revenues last year.
